title: make fails with pushd/popd errors
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
The most recent (as of 2021-01-16) version of podman (commit id: 73b036db566a8f3606f0b376728efe03fcf8685d ) does not build.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.   Clone the podman repo.  (per instructions)  

2.  make BUILDTAGS="selinux seccomp"

3. observe output as shown below

```
dave@coconut:~/go/src/github.com/containers/podman$ git log | head
commit 73b036db566a8f3606f0b376728efe03fcf8685d
Merge: 83ed464d2 47eeb02c7
Author: OpenShift Merge Robot <openshift-merge-robot@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Sat Jan 16 18:17:38 2021 -0500

    Merge pull request #8988 from Luap99/rename-port-completion-func
    
    [CI:DOCS] Rename AutocompletePortCommand func

commit 47eeb02c7a778890d102b89f9a352c664df1dda1
dave@coconut:~/go/src/github.com/containers/podman$ make BUILDTAGS="selinux seccomp"
Podman is being compiled without the systemd build tag. Install libsystemd on 	Ubuntu or systemd-devel on rpm based distro for journald support.
go build -mod=vendor  -gcflags 'all=-trimpath=/home/dave/go/src/github.com/containers/podman' -asmflags 'all=-trimpath=/home/dave/go/src/github.com/containers/podman' -ldflags '-X github.com/containers/podman/v2/libpod/define.gitCommit=73b036db566a8f3606f0b376728efe03fcf8685d -X github.com/containers/podman/v2/libpod/define.buildInfo=1610840294 -X github.com/containers/podman/v2/libpod/config._installPrefix=/usr/local -X github.com/containers/podman/v2/libpod/config._etcDir=/etc ' -tags "selinux seccomp" -o bin/podman ./cmd/podman
for i in pkg/bindings/system/types.go pkg/bindings/images/types.go pkg/bindings/play/types.go pkg/bindings/manifests/types.go pkg/bindings/volumes/types.go pkg/bindings/network/types.go pkg/bindings/containers/types.go pkg/bindings/generate/types.go pkg/bindings/pods/types.go; do \
	dirname=$(dirname ${i}); \
	shortname=$(basename ${dirname}); \
	pushd ${dirname}>/dev/null;  \
	echo ${dirname}; \
	echo go generate; \
	go generate; \
	popd > /dev/null; \
done;
/bin/sh: 4: pushd: not found
pkg/bindings/system
go generate
build .: cannot find module for path .
/bin/sh: 8: popd: not found
/bin/sh: 4: pushd: not found
pkg/bindings/images
go generate
build .: cannot find module for path .
/bin/sh: 8: popd: not found
/bin/sh: 4: pushd: not found
pkg/bindings/play
go generate
build .: cannot find module for path .
/bin/sh: 8: popd: not found
/bin/sh: 4: pushd: not found
pkg/bindings/manifests
go generate
build .: cannot find module for path .
/bin/sh: 8: popd: not found
/bin/sh: 4: pushd: not found
pkg/bindings/volumes
go generate
build .: cannot find module for path .
/bin/sh: 8: popd: not found
/bin/sh: 4: pushd: not found
pkg/bindings/network
go generate
build .: cannot find module for path .
/bin/sh: 8: popd: not found
/bin/sh: 4: pushd: not found
pkg/bindings/containers
go generate
build .: cannot find module for path .
/bin/sh: 8: popd: not found
/bin/sh: 4: pushd: not found
pkg/bindings/generate
go generate
build .: cannot find module for path .
/bin/sh: 8: popd: not found
/bin/sh: 4: pushd: not found
pkg/bindings/pods
go generate
build .: cannot find module for path .
/bin/sh: 8: popd: not found
make: *** [Makefile:465: .generate-bindings] Error 127
dave@coconut:~/go/src/github.com/containers/podman$ 



```


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This is on ubuntu 20.04 .   If I revert to the last version of podman from 2021-01-13, (b2ac2a3f10a8eaf9a2bc9040bacd3a2a94decbd0 ) the problem goes away.   For me, for now, this is an acceptable workaround.

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Ubuntu 20.04 physical intel.

comments.body:
` pushd` and `popd` are only available in bash, not in `/bin/sh` (i.e. dash)

So you need to use `make SHELL=/bin/bash`, when running on Debian

Broken in d498ef53aa71066aec7771ebb7316bf3eb3d4cde

Suppose that it could use e.g. `cd $dirname` and `cd -` instead ?
I think we can simplify a bit further.  Spinning up a PR in a bit.
